game_name: Super Star Force: Jikūreki no Himitsu

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn basic ship destruction and enemy patterns before ground exploration

element_introduction_order.element: Time travel mechanic (time units as fuel)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Non-linear progression requires understanding time point costs to reach different eras
Score management (max 99,990) becomes critical for dungeon transactions and time travel
Shift from vertical shmup to adventure/exploration gameplay with shoot 'em up elements
Each era contains concealed dungeons where time stones must be collected
Progressive collection gates access to final confrontation (DA 2137)
Item purchases and puzzle hints using accumulated score currency
Destroying egg in DA 1301 prevents Boolean Shang city-state elimination in DA 1608
Outcome determined by time travel decisions and time stone collection completeness

typical_relief_triggers: Landing from airborne combat to safe ground exploration
Discovering Norm's shop for item restoration
Completing a dungeon and collecting a time stone
Returning to free-cost DA 0001 era for resource gathering
typical_tension_triggers: Airborne combat with unique airborne enemies (DA 0820)
Dungeon exploration without clear exit paths
Time point depletion requiring mission restart
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cycle alternates between high-tension vertical scrolling flight segments and lower-tension ground exploration/puzzle phases; non-linear time travel allows player-driven pacing control but creates strategic tension around resource management and irreversible timeline decisions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden dungeons accessible from main level areas; optional time periods unlocked by time point expenditure and completion state
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Underground entrances accessed via volcano craters (DA 0001); closed gates requiring specific items (Statue of Dorera key for DA 0820); submerged pathways in oceanic eras (DA 1301)

difficulty_curve.early_game: DA 0001 (Erujinba) and DA 0316 (Genesis) introduce fundamental shmup combat and basic dungeon navigation; no time point cost to return to DA 0001
difficulty_curve.mid_game: DA 0820 (The Dawn) marks significant difficulty spike with unique airborne enemies and mandatory item acquisition before final stage access; DA 1003 and DA 1301 introduce complex environmental puzzles and temporal consequence mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: DA 1608 (Ice Age) and DA 2137 (final confrontation with Gordess) demand mastery of timeline manipulation, full resource management, and time stone collection; multiple endings based on cumulative decisions
notable_spikes: DA 0820 (The Dawn) - unique enemy types and gated progression
DA 1301 (Ending Hills Era) - first major temporal consequence (Ganimu egg destruction)
DA 2137 (final boss) - Gordess encounter requires complete time stone collection and optimal timeline state

notable_level_patterns.description: Each era begins with vertical-scrolling airborne combat, concludes with mandatory ground-based dungeon exploration to acquire time stone or key item; establishes rhythm of action followed by puzzle-solving
Each time period has distinct visual/thematic environment (volcano, mountain, forest, ocean, ice) that communicates era identity and contains environment-specific enemies and mechanics
Actions in earlier eras (cause) directly affect later eras (effect); players must understand causal chains to optimize progression path and endings
notable_level_patterns.example_level: DA 1301 Ganimu egg → DA 1608 Boolean Shang fate

notable_level_patterns.description: DA 2010 acts as central hub; time travel allows player to visit eras in nearly any order (gated by time point cost and progression prerequisites); encourages exploration and experimentation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Free return to DA 0001 vs. escalating costs for distant eras

sources_used: wikipedia: Super Star Force: Jikūreki no Himitsu
